display.purpose: Screen titles, reflective prompts. Warm literary serif for affect-labeling moments.

body.purpose: Interface copy, prose, controls. Humanist system sans.

mono.purpose: PHQ-9 scores, P/M values, XP counters — tabular-nums.

shadows.name: resting
shadows.purpose: Default elevation for panels, primary buttons, slider thumbs.

shadows.name: lifted
shadows.purpose: Genuinely floating surfaces only: XP reward toast, modal-adjacent sheets.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: The only easing curve in the system. No bounce, no elastic. ~160-220ms.

components.name: Primary Button
components.css: .ds-btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; min-height: 48px; padding: 0 24px; border-radius: 999px; border: 1px solid transparent; font-weight: 600; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; transition: transform 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), background 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(0.55 0.10 205); color: oklch(0.99 0.005 205); box-shadow: 0 1px 2px oklch(0.4 0.02 240 / 0.06), 0 1px 3px oklch(0.4 0.02 240 / 0.08); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.48 0.11 205); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2.5px solid oklch(0.55 0.10 205); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary/tertiary action, transparent with a hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 48px; padding: 0 24px; border-radius: 999px; background: transparent; border: 1px solid oklch(0.90 0.006 220); color: oklch(0.24 0.02 240); font-weight: 600; transition: background 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(0.965 0.006 220); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2.5px solid oklch(0.55 0.10 205); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Panel
components.description: The base content surface. Never nested inside another panel.
components.html:
This week

Your mood has been steadier than last week, especially after evening walks.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: oklch(1 0 0); border: 1px solid oklch(0.90 0.006 220); border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px oklch(0.4 0.02 240 / 0.06), 0 1px 3px oklch(0.4 0.02 240 / 0.08); font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; } .ds-panel-title { font-family: Georgia, 'Iowan Old Style', 'Times New Roman', serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.12rem; color: oklch(0.24 0.02 240); margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-panel-body { color: oklch(0.46 0.02 240); line-height: 1.55; }

components.name: Selected Pill
components.description: Selectable chip/pill for values and starter activities, with a selected state.
components.html: <button class="ds-pill is-selected" aria-pressed="true">Morning walk</button><button class="ds-pill" aria-pressed="false">Journaling</button>
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 999px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.90 0.006 220); background: oklch(1 0 0); color: oklch(0.24 0.02 240); font-weight: 500; min-height: 44px; margin-right: 8px; transition: background 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), border-color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-pill.is-selected { background: oklch(0.95 0.03 205); border-color: oklch(0.55 0.10 205); color: oklch(0.48 0.11 205); font-weight: 600; } .ds-pill:focus-visible { outline: 2.5px solid oklch(0.55 0.10 205); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Bottom Tab Bar
components.description: Thumb-reachable four-tab navigation; the Safety tab is always coral, active or not.
components.html: TodayLogInsightsSafety
components.css: .ds-tabbar { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); gap: 4px; padding: 8px 12px; background: color-mix(in oklch, oklch(1 0 0) 88%, transparent); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border-top: 1px solid oklch(0.90 0.006 220); } .ds-tab { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 54px; border-radius: 10px; text-decoration: none; color: oklch(0.46 0.02 240); font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 600; transition: color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), background 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-tab.is-active { color: oklch(0.48 0.11 205); background: oklch(0.95 0.03 205); } .ds-tab-safety { color: oklch(0.55 0.16 25); }

narrative.northStar: A calm friend in a dim room
narrative.overview: Companion is built for one recurring scene: late evening, low light, a tired person on the couch tapping to log how they feel. Every design decision is tested against that scene — not "does this look good in a portfolio," but "does this feel kind to someone with no energy to spare, possibly at 1am, possibly in crisis." The system reads as a steady, well-trained friend: warm and plain-spoken, never clinical-cold, never chirpy-wellness. This is explicitly not a clinical/medical dashboard and explicitly not a chirpy wellness app or gamified habit tracker. Color strategy is restrained: quiet neutral surfaces, one calm teal primary, one warm accent reserved for human/hope moments. The four mood-quadrant hues are a small, deliberate data palette, not decoration — the only place the system spends color freely.
keyCharacteristics: Restrained color, spent deliberately (one primary, one accent, four data hues)
Literary serif for reflection, humanist sans for interface, mono for numbers
Soft geometry throughout — no sharp corners, no side-stripe accents
Ease-out-only motion, never bouncy, always reduced-motion-safe
Safety is ambient furniture, never a modal, never gated

rules.name: The Rarity Rule
rules.body: Ember accent appears only at moments of genuine reward or encouragement — never as decoration, never as a structural color. If it's on screen and nothing good just happened, remove it.

rules.name: The No Color-Only Rule
rules.body: Every quadrant and safety signal pairs its color with position, a label, and an icon. A screen that only a sighted, color-typical user can read is a broken screen, not a finished one.

rules.name: The Calm Ceiling Rule
rules.body: Display clamp max stays ≤ 2.2rem in-app. This product never needs to shout to be heard.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat or resting-shadow at rest. Nothing gets the lifted shadow just for looking important; it's earned by actually floating over content.

dos: Do use OKLCH for every color token; keep the primary hue at 205° and the accent at 45° so new colors stay in-family.
Do pair every quadrant/safety color with position, label, and icon — never color alone.
Do cap in-app display type at clamp(1.6rem, 1.2rem + 2vw, 2.2rem) (≤2.2rem); this is a companion app, not a landing page.
Do keep tap targets ≥44px and focus rings visible (2.5px solid var(--primary), 2px offset) for low-dexterity, low-energy use.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion: reduce fallback (instant placement or crossfade) — required, not optional.
Do keep the Safety route always reachable from the app bar and the tab bar, ungated, unconditional.
Do separate sections with space and a single hairline rather than nested boxes.
donts: Don't use gradient text, ever — this is one of PRODUCT.md's named AI-slop anti-references.
Don't use a border-left/border-right stripe greater than 1px as a colored accent (no side-stripe borders).
Don't use glassmorphism as a default treatment — the blurred tab bar is the one deliberate exception; it doesn't spread further.
Don't build identical card grids or nest a card inside a card.
Don't put a tiny tracked-uppercase eyebrow above every section, or numbered section markers (01 / 02 / 03) as default scaffolding.
Don't shame streaks or punish missed days — missed days get "welcome back," not guilt.
Don't make points/XP the point — gamification is decoration for a reward already earned from the data, never a manipulation layer.
Don't gate or add confirmation friction to the Safety route, and don't style it as a flashing alarm.
Don't rely on color alone anywhere valence/quadrant meaning is encoded.
